evals.name: architecture-decision
evals.prompt: we're debating whether to split our Django monolith into microservices — we have 200k daily users, a team of 8, and the main pain point is deploy times for the checkout module. I want a deep independent analysis of the trade-offs
evals.expected_output: Should formulate a clear, context-rich question for Codex including the constraints (200k users, 8-person team, checkout deploy pain), then launch codex-oracle.py in background.

assertions.text: Includes key constraints in the Codex question
assertions.description: Question to Codex should mention the specific constraints: 200k daily users, team of 8, checkout module deploy times
codex-oracle.py should be launched with run_in_background: true as Codex can take up to 30 minutes

assertions.text: Ends turn after launching, does not start parallel analysis
assertions.description: Skill rule: do not start your own parallel analysis while Codex runs — Codex is the analyst

evals.name: elusive-concurrency-bug
evals.prompt: there's an intermittent bug where our Celery job queue processes the same job twice — it only happens under high concurrency with 50+ workers and we can't reproduce it locally. The job processing code is in app/tasks/processor.py
evals.expected_output: Should craft a focused debugging question for Codex, including the file path and concurrency details.

assertions.text: Question includes the specific file path
assertions.description: Should mention app/tasks/processor.py in the question to point Codex in the right direction

assertions.text: Question includes concurrency context (50+ workers, Celery)
assertions.description: Should mention the Celery queue, 50+ workers, and duplicate processing symptom

assertions.text: Does not attempt to debug the issue itself
assertions.description: Oracle delegates deep analysis to Codex — should not read files or grep for the bug pattern before Codex responds

evals.name: security-assessment
evals.prompt: assess our JWT token refresh strategy — we use 15-min access tokens and 7-day refresh tokens stored in httpOnly cookies. I'm worried about token theft and replay attack scenarios. The auth code is in src/auth/tokens.ts
evals.expected_output: Should formulate a security-focused question for Codex including token lifetimes, storage mechanism, and file path.

assertions.description: Should mention 15-min access tokens, 7-day refresh tokens, httpOnly cookies in the Codex question

assertions.text: Question frames security concerns (theft, replay attacks)
assertions.description: Should specifically ask Codex to assess token theft and replay attack scenarios

assertions.text: Includes file path hint for Codex
assertions.description: Should mention src/auth/tokens.ts to help Codex find the relevant code
